canonical_action: isolate each shard's sync work in its own spawned subprocess, so the OS reclaims the shard's memory at process exit and the daemon's resident footprint drops
closure_reason: during bulk-resync the fan-in IPC serialization tripled sync latency, and peak memory moved to the coordinator process that buffers every shard's fan-in — the limit was still crossed, just in a different process
measured_outcome: piloted on one shard class: sync latency ~3x during bulk-resync, and peak RSS relocated to the coordinator and still crossed the cgroup limit; abandoned
